title: Artifact Registry Push/Pull and Vulnerability Scan
dataSources: Audit logs (Artifact Registry API), Container Analysis (vulnerability occurrences)
spl:
index=gcp sourcetype="google:gcp:pubsub:message" protoPayload.serviceName="artifactregistry.googleapis.com"
| spath output=method path=protoPayload.methodName
| stats count by method resource.labels.repository
| sort -count
description: Unusual push/pull may indicate abuse. Vulnerability scan findings in images require remediation before deployment.
value: Unusual push/pull may indicate abuse. Vulnerability scan findings in images require remediation before deployment.
implementation: Forward Artifact Registry audit logs via Pub/Sub. Ingest Container Analysis for CVE findings. Alert on critical/high in production repos. Baseline push/pull by principal; alert on anomalies.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
‚Ä¢ Install and configure the required add-on or app: `Splunk_TA_google-cloudplatform`.
‚Ä¢ Ensure the following data sources are available: Audit logs (Artifact Registry API), Container Analysis (vulnerability occurrences).
‚Ä¢ For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 ‚Äî Configure data collection
Forward Artifact Registry audit logs via Pub/Sub. Ingest Container Analysis for CVE findings. Alert on critical/high in production repos. Baseline push/pull by principal; alert on anomalies.

Step 2 ‚Äî Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=gcp sourcetype="google:gcp:pubsub:message" protoPayload.serviceName="artifactregistry.googleapis.com"
| spath output=method path=protoPayload.methodName
| stats count by method resource.labels.repository
| sort -count
```

Understanding this SPL

**Artifact Registry Push/Pull and Vulnerability Scan** ‚Äî Unusual push/pull may indicate abuse. Vulnerability scan findings in images require remediation before deployment.

Documented **Data sources**: Audit logs (Artifact Registry API), Container Analysis (vulnerability occurrences). **App/TA** (typical add-on context): `Splunk_TA_google-cloudplatform`. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed‚Äîrename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: gcp; **sourcetype**: google:gcp:pubsub:message. If that sourcetype is not mentioned in Data sources, double-check parsing or update the documentation to match the feed you actually ingest.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

‚Ä¢ Scopes the data: index=gcp, sourcetype="google:gcp:pubsub:message". Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
‚Ä¢ Extracts structured paths (JSON/XML) with `spath`.
‚Ä¢ `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by method resource.labels.repository** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions (useful for per-host, per-user, or per-entity comparisons for this use case).
‚Ä¢ Orders rows with `sort` ‚Äî combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.


Step 3 ‚Äî Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with known good/bad scenarios if applicable. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 ‚Äî Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (repo, method, count), Bar chart (top push/pull), Table (image, CVE, severity).
visualization: Table (repo, method, count), Bar chart (top push/pull), Table (image, CVE, severity).
